refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable monitoring, controlling, and optimizing energy consumption to meet the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad; decomposing it into monitoring, control, and optimization clarifies how energy efficiency can be achieved systematically.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify by measuring energy_per_ton over the evaluation period and confirming it meets ≤ E_target; validate monitoring data accuracy, control actions, and optimization effectiveness.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor energy consumption and tonnage moved in real-time.
FR2: Control operational parameters to reduce energy usage.
FR3: Optimize task scheduling and equipment usage for energy efficiency.

candidate_dps: DP1: Deploy energy and tonnage sensors with telemetry data aggregation.
DP2: Implement control algorithms adjusting vehicle operation parameters.
DP3: Use task scheduling software to optimize equipment usage.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1, DP2, and DP3 combined to address monitoring, control, and optimization respectively.

potential_sources: Data dependency between monitoring and control modules.
Scheduling optimization depends on control feedback.
coupling_check.notes: The DPs are logically coupled but can be modularized with well-defined interfaces to minimize tight coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes reliable sensor data and network connectivity.
Risk of control algorithm not adapting well to dynamic conditions.
Optimization effectiveness depends on accurate task data.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Ensure modular design with clear interface contracts to manage coupling and allow independent verification.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system integration including energy monitoring, control, and optimization functions.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Used for real-time energy and tonnage data exchange between Vehicle and NGHE Solution.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives task schedules and operational commands from Teleoperation or Site Manager.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends alerts to Safety Services if energy usage indicates unsafe operation.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports communication between NGHE Solution, Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services.

module_containment_scores.notes: Centralizes energy efficiency logic and data processing; low diffusion risk.
Contains sensors and actuators but limited control logic to avoid duplication.
Primarily for remote control; minimal energy efficiency logic.
Focuses on safety alerts; not responsible for energy efficiency control.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Energy efficiency functions are best contained within the NGHE Solution module to centralize logic and minimize diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential bottleneck if NGHE Solution becomes too complex.
Dependency on reliable network links for telemetry and tasking.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Monitor complexity growth in NGHE Solution; consider splitting optimization into submodules if needed.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FRs are well matched by selected DPs covering monitoring, control, and optimization with clear logical mapping.

diffusion_risks: Low diffusion risk due to centralized containment in NGHE Solution.
Interfaces are well defined and limited to known interfaces.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support NGHE Solution as the primary module; Vehicle and others have limited scope.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by monitoring telemetry data and validating control and optimization outcomes within NGHE Solution.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of FRs to NGHE Solution module with well-defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Refine interface specifications for telemetry and tasking to ensure robustness.
Plan for scalability in NGHE Solution to handle complexity.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition and mapping from FR to DP to IM is consistent, complete, and verifiable with acceptable risk levels.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor energy consumption and tonnage moved in real-time.
FR2: Control operational parameters to reduce energy usage.
FR3: Optimize task scheduling and equipment usage for energy efficiency.

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Reliable sensor data and network connectivity.
Modular design will manage coupling effectively.
open_risks: Complexity growth in NGHE Solution module.
Control algorithm adaptability to dynamic conditions.
evidence_needed: Verification of interface robustness and data integrity.
